title: Kaitabheshvara Temple, Kubatur
tagline: Kaitabheshvara Temple, Kubatur — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: The Kaitabheshvara temple (also spelt Kaitabhesvara or Kaitabheshwara, known also as Kotisvara) is located in the town of Kubatur (also spelt Kubattur or Kuppattur, and called Kunt
overview.description: The Kaitabheshvara temple (also spelt Kaitabhesvara or Kaitabheshwara, known also as Kotisvara) is located in the town of Kubatur (also spelt Kubattur or Kuppattur, and called Kuntalanagara or Kotipura in ancient inscriptions), near Anavatti in the Shimoga district of Karnataka state, India. The temple was constructed during the reign of Hoysala King Vinayaditya around 1100 AD. The Hoysala ruling family was during this time a powerful feudatory of the imperial Western Chalukya Empire ruled by King Vikramaditya VI. topPlaces.name: Anavatti
topPlaces.description: Anavatti is a town in the Shivamogga district of Karnataka, India. Anavatti was previously known as Ane Hatti (transl. elephants sanctuary) during the Kadamba dynasty.

topPlaces.name: Agasanahalli (Sorab)
topPlaces.description: Agasanahalli is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Sorab taluk of Shimoga district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Dandavati
topPlaces.description: Dandavati is a river that flows through Sorab, India. It originates from a reservoir in Kattinakere.

topPlaces.name: Tumarikoppa
topPlaces.description: Tumarikoppa is a village in Dharwad district of Karnataka, India.

items.note: Anavatti is a town in the Shivamogga district of Karnataka, India. Anavatti was previously known as Ane Hatti (transl. elephants sanctuary) during the Kadamba dynasty.
Agasanahalli is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Sorab taluk of Shimoga district in Karnataka.
Dandavati is a river that flows through Sorab, India. It originates from a reservoir in Kattinakere.
Tumarikoppa is a village in Dharwad district of Karnataka, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kaitabheshvara Temple, Kubatur?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kaitabheshvara Temple, Kubatur is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kaitabheshvara Temple, Kubatur?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Hubli Airport (88 km) and the nearest railway station is Hubballi Junction (86 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kaitabheshvara Temple, Kubatur?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kaitabheshvara Temple, Kubatur at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kaitabheshvara Temple, Kubatur?
faq.a: Stays in Kaitabheshvara Temple, Kubatur start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kaitabheshvara Temple, Kubatur?
faq.a: The top attractions are Anavatti, Agasanahalli (Sorab), Dandavati, Tumarikoppa.
